title: Building a Client-Side Adaptive UX Engine
description: How I built NeuroAdaptive UX: a pure client-side interaction speed and fatigue detector with React and Framer Motion.
content: Standard interfaces are static, failing to adapt to user fatigue or stress. In this article, I break down the mathematical modeling of typing cadence (rolling average WPM and correction rate) and cursor jitter calculation on the main thread. By processing pointer coordinates through a moving variance window, we can determine the cognitive load of a user locally and adjust layouts asynchronously to reduce drop-out rates without leaking any interaction packets to the server.

title: Secure Distributed Voting with JWT & MongoDB
description: Engineering an atomic transaction lifecycle to guarantee exactly-once voting rules under concurrent request stress tests.
content: Preventing concurrent double-voting is one of the hardest challenges in web-scale digital voting. Standard web servers run in parallel, meaning a voter sending twin requests milliseconds apart can trick the backend. In this case study, I detail the implementation of a compound index in MongoDB combined with atomic transactional locks to guarantee that once the voter state flags transition, duplicate requests are discarded instantly.
